risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "A research pharmacist following a computer-generated block randomization table coordinated randomization and treatment assignment." Comment: This explicitly describes a computer-generated block randomization table, which is a proper random allocation method. The additional context that a research pharmacist coordinated assignment further supports that the sequence was generated systematically rather than predictably.

_evidence.method_quote: A research pharmacist following a computer-generated block randomization table coordinated randomization and treatment assignment.
_evidence.method_summary: Randomization was coordinated by a research pharmacist using a computer-generated block randomization table.
context_clues: two-arm, randomized, multicenter, open-label, controlled, intention-to-treat trial

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "A research pharmacist following a computer-generated block randomization table coordinated randomization and treatment assignment." Comment: This explicitly indicates that a research pharmacist coordinated the randomization and treatment assignment, which functions as an allocation-concealment mechanism by keeping recruiters from knowing the next assignment before enrollment; no foreseeability signal is described.

_evidence.concealment_quote: A research pharmacist following a computer-generated block randomization table coordinated randomization and treatment assignment.
_evidence.note: The article describes sequence generation and that a research pharmacist coordinated randomization and treatment assignment, but it does not mention envelopes or a central allocation system.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "This randomized open-label clinical trial included patients with and without diabetes undergoing primary, elective, and emergency CABG who experienced perioperative hyperglycemia" and "The study was a two-arm, randomized, multicenter, open-label, controlled, intention-to-treat trial." Comment: The trial explicitly states it was open-label, and the intervention was different glucose targets/insulin management, so participants and personnel were not blinded. Because outcomes included perioperative complications, infections, readmissions, and emergency room visits, knowledge of allocation could plausibly influence care and outcome ascertainment across the study.

_evidence.blinding_quote: This randomized open-label clinical trial included patients with and without diabetes undergoing primary, elective, and emergency CABG who experienced perioperative hyperglycemia, defined as a BG >140 mg/dL.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "A research pharmacist following a computer-generated block randomization table coordinated randomization and treatment assignment." Comment: The reported outcomes are all objective/record-based measures (hospital mortality, wound infection, bacteremia, respiratory failure, pneumonia, acute kidney injury, MACE, hypoglycemia thresholds, length of stay, readmissions, ER visits), and the paper does not describe any subjective clinician-rated or patient-reported outcome assessment. Given the objective nature of the outcomes, lack of explicit blinding of the outcome assessor is unlikely to bias measurement in this study.

outcomes.label: sternal wound infection / surgical wound infection

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "A total of 305 patients were randomized; of them, 1 patient in the intensive group and 2 patients in the conservative group withdrew after randomization but prior to receiving insulin treatment. A total of 151 patients in the intensive group and 151 in the conservative group were included in the final analysis." Comment: Post-randomization attrition was minimal (3/305; about 1.0%) and balanced across arms (1 vs 2 withdrawals). The withdrawals occurred before receiving insulin treatment, and the study reports an intention-to-treat trial with complete final analyzed numbers in both groups, so incomplete outcome data are unlikely to bias the outcomes of interest.

per_arm_flow.reasons: “1 patient in the intensive group … withdrew after randomization but prior to receiving insulin treatment.”
“2 patients in the conservative group withdrew after randomization but prior to receiving insulin treatment.”

_evidence.notes: Attrition numbers are for the randomized trial overall; final analysis included 151 per arm. Withdrawals occurred after randomization but before receiving insulin treatment.
